page.hint: This page is for AI agents. The top-level window.__partiAgent exposes getState()/describe()/action() and more, callable from the console or a headless browser evaluate; the text below mirrors it for screenshots or DOM reading.

app.documentTitle: Parti — Create and play together with friends

header.tagline: Create and play together with friends

settings.title: User settings
settings.description: This identity is saved in your browser and used when creating or joining rooms.
settings.sheetDescription: Manage your local identity and interface preferences.
settings.profileDescription: This identity is saved in your browser and used when creating or joining rooms.
settings.transportDescription: Choose the connection service for the next online room you create.
settings.transportHint: Only affects newly created rooms; invite links always determine how a room is joined.

peerjs.title: Default connection
peerjs.summary: Best for most remote sessions with friends. Try this first.
peerjs.network: Works between Wi-Fi and mobile data connections.
peerjs.latency: Usually low latency for more responsive play.
peerjs.reliability: Some restricted networks may fail to connect; try Stable connection if that happens.

supabase.title: Stable connection
supabase.summary: Use when the default cannot connect or players have complex network conditions.
supabase.network: Works across Wi-Fi, mobile data, and other network types.
supabase.latency: Traffic is relayed by a realtime service, so latency is usually higher.
supabase.reliability: Does not rely on NAT traversal, improving connection success on restricted networks.

lan.title: Local network
lan.summary: Best when everyone is in the same place and on the same Wi-Fi or LAN.
lan.network: Local network only; it cannot connect across the internet or mobile data.
lan.latency: Devices communicate over the local network, usually giving the lowest latency.
lan.reliability: Does not rely on an external realtime service and is ideal for playing together in person.

settings.profilesHint: For advanced users who need to connect to a self-hosted service.
settings.profilesDescription: Manage PeerJS, LAN, or Supabase backends used for new online rooms. Built-in configurations are read-only.
settings.nameHint: Friends will recognize you by this name in rooms.
settings.saved: Saved. It will apply on your next visit or reconnect.
settings.languageHint: Interface language, saved in this browser.
settings.donationDescription: If Parti has been useful to you, you can buy the developer a coffee and support its maintenance and continued development.
settings.donationDialogDescription: Thank you for supporting Parti! Parti is currently maintained by a single developer, and your donation will support maintenance and continued development.
settings.clearDataDescription: This will permanently delete all data saved in this browser (including your profile, templates, rooms, and replays). This cannot be undone. Continue?

hero.title: Online lobby
hero.description: Discover rooms in progress, or create a fun space for your group.

join.invalidLink: This does not look like a valid room invite link.
join.scanSheetDescription: Point your camera at the invite QR code to join the room.
join.cameraDenied: Camera access was denied. Check your browser permissions.
join.scanFailed: Could not start the scanner. Please try again.

lan.title: On your network
lan.loading: Looking for rooms on your network…
lan.offlineDescription: The LAN signaling server could not be reached. Online rooms and other sync methods still work.
lan.emptyDescription: LAN rooms created by devices sharing your public network will appear here.

offline.title: Lobby is temporarily unavailable
offline.description: You can still create a room and invite friends with a link.

empty.title: Waiting for the first party
empty.description: No public rooms yet. Create one and invite your friends.

editor.title: Create online room
editor.description: Pick a template to start building, then invite friends when you're ready.
editor.aiCreateDescription: Can’t find the game you want? Let AI build one.

library.emptyDescription: Try another category or import a room above.

market.description: Online room templates published by the community via GitHub. Install one to create a room.
market.publishGuide: Learn how to publish your room →
market.emptyTitle: No rooms in the market yet
market.emptyDescription: Rooms published by community authors and approved by maintainers will appear here.
market.staleWarning: Failed to refresh the market. Showing previously cached content.
market.manifestUnavailable: Package info not written yet; waiting for the registry check
market.manifestInvalid: Package info invalid; cannot install right now
market.downloadFallbackHint: After the download finishes, use “Import from ZIP” above to install it.
market.releaseOnlyHint: Only a validated release ZIP is available. Download it, then use “Import from ZIP” above.

newRoom.title: New room
newRoom.githubPlaceholder: Paste a GitHub URL and press Enter

template.replaceDescription: Switching to "{name}" will replace your code and extra files. This cannot be undone.

files.extraDescription: Add styles or other text assets. They are saved with the room.

error.invalidJson: Room config is not valid JSON. Please check and try again.
error.clipboardFailed: Copy failed. Please allow clipboard access for this page and try again.

dock.readyDescription: After creating, you can set title, password, and visibility.

ai.dialogTitle: Let AI build your game idea
ai.dialogDescription: Copy a Parti-ready prompt for your favorite AI. It will read the project docs first, then generate complete room code.
ai.filesTitle: AI will generate these required files
ai.step1: Copy the prompt and send it to your AI.
ai.step2: Add gameplay, player count, win conditions, and visual style.
ai.step3: When AI finishes, click “AI already generated — add now”, then use “Quick import AI result” on the editor page to paste the full reply into the three files.
ai.warning: AI-generated code may still have bugs. Review before importing and validate with local preview.
ai.prompt:
Please help me create a multiplayer online game room that can be imported directly into Parti.

Before designing and writing code, read and understand all documents in this GitHub docs directory:
https://github.com/glink25/Parti/tree/main/docs

Strictly follow the docs on Manifest, client API, Worker API, protocol, and Host Runtime. Room state must be authoritative on the Worker; clients may only read state and submit actions through the Parti API.

If critical details are missing, ask brief necessary questions first. Once you have enough info, the final result must:
1. Provide a complete file structure with at least parti.room.json, index.html, and room.worker.js.
2. Output full contents of all three files—no ellipses, pseudocode, placeholders, or unsupported dependencies.
3. Ensure manifest entry files, player counts, and permissions match the code.
4. Implement multiplayer sync, server-side action validation, win/end conditions, and safe handling of bad input.
5. UI should be clear, responsive, and usable on mobile.
6. Self-review against all Parti docs before output.

Format the final answer as Markdown with one fenced code block per file. The fence info string MUST be the filename so the user can one-click import:

### parti.room.json
```parti.room.json
(full JSON contents)
```

### index.html
```index.html
(full HTML contents)
```

### room.worker.js
```room.worker.js
(full worker code)
```

After all code blocks, explicitly tell the user outside the code fences that the game code is ready and they can copy your entire response, return to Parti, and paste it into the “Quick import AI result” box.

My game idea (please fill in gameplay, player count, win/lose conditions, and visual style):
[Describe your game idea here]

aiImport.title: Import AI result
aiImport.description: Paste the AI’s full reply. We parse Markdown code fences and fill parti.room.json, index.html, and room.worker.js.
aiImport.empty: Paste or type the AI reply first.
aiImport.parseFailed: Could not find complete files: {files}. Make sure the reply includes Markdown fences named after each file.

defaults.blankDescription: Start from a simple interactive counter and shape it into your game.
defaults.roomDescription: An interactive room to play with friends
defaults.html:
My room

0

loading.browserHint: For the best experience, use your system browser or Chrome

join.passwordDescription: This room requires a password to join.
join.leaveDescription: You will return to the lobby and disconnect from this session.

error.title: Unable to enter room
error.connectionFailed: Cannot reach the host. Try the steps below, then retry.
error.tipNetwork: Make sure your network connection is stable
error.tipSameWifi: Try joining the same Wi‑Fi network as the host
error.tipSyncMethod: Ask the host to switch Sync method in User Settings and recreate the room
error.tipHostOnline: Confirm the host is still in the room and that you are using the latest invite link

fetchPackage.timeout: Timed out fetching room package (host may be offline or using an older version)
fetchPackage.disconnected: Disconnected from host before receiving the room package

invite.title: Join the room together
invite.withPassword: The link includes the room password and is ready to share.
invite.withoutPassword: Copy the link and invite friends to join now.
invite.inviteAiHint: Copy a prompt that lets an AI agent join and play through a headless browser.
invite.joinHelpBodyPeerjs: This room connects players directly to each other, which depends heavily on network conditions. Firewalls, carriers, or local network restrictions can cause failed joins, disconnects, or noticeable lag.
invite.joinHelpBodyLan: This room is discoverable through a LocalSend-compatible signaling server and sends game data directly over WebRTC. Devices must be grouped under the same public IP or IPv6 /64, and restrictive networks can prevent joining.
invite.joinHelpBodySupabase: This room relays data through a cloud server, which is usually easier to join across networks, but you still need a stable connection.
invite.joinHelpSuggestion: Leave this room, go back to the home screen, open User Settings, switch Sync method, then create a new room and share the new invite link. Note: the sync method only applies to rooms you create afterward.

settings.title: Manage room
settings.lanVisibleHint: Visible to Parti devices grouped on this public network.
settings.lanHiddenHint: Hidden from discovery; existing invite links on this network can still join.
settings.replayDescription: Starts from the current state and stays on this device.
settings.sheetDescription: Invite friends or adjust title, password, and visibility.

share.sheetDescription: Copy the link or QR code to invite friends.

status.unsupported: This browser or device does not support the motion sensors required by the room.
status.needs-permission: This room uses device tilt controls. Select the button to grant sensor access.
status.requesting: Waiting for the browser permission decision.
status.active: Sensors are enabled and the room can read device orientation.
status.denied: Permission was denied. Allow motion and orientation access in the browser site settings.
status.blocked-by-policy: Sensors are blocked by an insecure connection or the site's permissions policy.
status.no-data: Permission is enabled. Waiting for sensor data from the device.

replays.title: Local replays
replays.description: Host replays stored only in this browser.
replays.empty: No saved replays yet. Enable recording in room settings to create one.
replays.missing: This replay could not be found or has been deleted.

local.loading: Loading room package and starting runtime…
local.title: Local multiplayer preview · {name}

error.MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND: parti.room.json not found. This does not look like a valid room package.
error.MANIFEST_INVALID: parti.room.json is not a valid room manifest.
error.ENTRY_MISSING: The room package is missing a manifest entry file: {path}
error.NO_COMPLETE_PACKAGE: No package in the requested scope has both a valid manifest and every declared entry file.
error.SOURCE_PATH_INVALID: The room package contains an unsafe or non-canonical path: {path}
error.SOURCE_READ_FAILED: Failed to read a room package file: {path}
error.ZIP_INVALID: The file is not a readable ZIP room package.
error.GITHUB_URL_INVALID: Unrecognized GitHub URL. Provide a repository, tree folder, or blob file link.
error.GITHUB_REF_NOT_FOUND: The branch or tag in this GitHub URL does not exist.
error.GITHUB_RATE_LIMITED: GitHub API rate limited (60/hour unauthenticated). Please try again later.
error.GITHUB_TREE_FAILED: Failed to read the GitHub repository tree ({status}).
error.GITHUB_TREE_FAILED_UNKNOWN: Failed to read the GitHub repository tree. Check your connection and try again.
error.GITHUB_TREE_TRUNCATED: GitHub truncated the repository tree, so a complete room package cannot be located safely.
error.GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_FAILED: Failed to download a room package file from GitHub: {path}
error.GITHUB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND: parti.room.json not found in the folder for this URL.
error.RELEASE_NOT_FOUND: No matching GitHub release was found.
error.RELEASE_ASSET_MISSING: The release does not contain parti.room.zip.
error.RELEASE_MANUAL_REQUIRED: One-click import from the branch failed. Try downloading the Release ZIP, then install it with “Import from ZIP”.

error.REGISTRY_FETCH_FAILED: Failed to load the room market ({status}). Please try again later.
error.REGISTRY_RATE_LIMITED: GitHub API rate limit reached. The room market cannot refresh right now, please try again later.
